english: ćan (can), sùl-ćan (sul can) a channeled pillar. — ka-γżu (ka gzhu) capital of a pillar. — ka-γżu-γduń (ka gzhu gdung) beam of the capital (pillars are mostly made of wood).
prob. a sort of gourd Wdn.
1. alabaster Sch. — 2. n. of a country.
n. of a famous ancient pandita or Brahmanical scholar.
is said to denote a sort of alabaster or of steatite in C.
n. of a fabulous, very smooth, stuff or cloth, Gyatch.
Ld. *ka-sam* (*ka sam*), prob. a sort of oats; differing from yug-po (yug po), accounted superior to buckwheat, but inferior to wheat.
title of a book cited in Glr.
(kwa-ye (kwa ye)) oh! holla! hear! so e. g. at the beginning of a royal proclamation Pth.

C. & B. sugar; śel-ka-ra (shel ka ra) crystallized s., sugar-candy, rgyál-mo-ka-ra (rgyal mo ka ra) id. Sch.; byé-ma-ka-ra (bye ma ka ra) ground sugar. — ka-ra-ja (ka ra ja) tea with sugar; Sch. 'a sweet soup'? — ka-ra tog-tóg (ka ra tog tog) sugar in lumps. — kara-śiń (ka ra shing) sugar-cane. (W. kǎ-ra (ka ra)).
also ka-ra-wi-ra (ka ra wi ra) (Ssk.) oleander flower, Nerium odorum.
Ssk. a medicinal fruit, Galedupa arborea.
Pth. more correctly ka-raṇ-ḍa (ka raN Da), Ssk., a species of duck.
wedge.
a fabulous place or country in the north of Asia; also n. of a grammar Cs.
Cs.: 'Ssk., n. of a bird', Will.: 'kalāpin peacock; the Indian cuckoo'.
Ssk. pitcher, jar.
W. mud, mixture of earth and water used instead of clay (C. & B.: jim-pa ('jim pa)); the word is also used for other similar compounds.
Ssk. n. of a bird.
1. skull Lex. — 2. = ka-lé (ka le) W.
the Tib. alphabet, v. ka (ka).
Ssk. n. of different tracts in the eastern part of India; Sch.: 'Korea', without giving further explanation; perh. Mongol writers call it so?
Ar. كالَب bullet-mould W.
also ka-léb (ka leb), saddle-cloth.
Ssk. a sort of grass, Saccharum spontaneum; Tibetans often seem to mistake it for ku-śa (ku sha) q. v.
Ssk., adj. of Kāsi (Banāras): inhabitant of Banāras; ka-śi-kai (ka shi kai) ras, a sort of fine cottoncloth.

also *ká-so, kas* (*ka so, kas*) (perh. a mutilated form of bka-stsal (bka' stsal)) resp. yes, Sir! very well, Sir! at your service! (W. also: *ká-sa-ju* (*ka sa ju*) v. żu (zhu)) W. frq., also C. u. f., never in B.
suddenly Sch.
mischief, harm, injury Cs.
Ssk. heron.
n. of a terrifying deity Glr., prob. = kiń-káń (king kang), which is said to signify Rāhula (v. sgra-γćan (sgra gcan) & drag-γśed (drag gshed) in drag-pa (drag pa)).
Ssk. ('thorny') Wilson: Solanum Jaquini; in Lh. a sort of wild Rubus.
Ld. sometimes instead of the affix ka (ka), e. g. γnyis-kád (gnyis kad), tsan-kád (tsan kad); perh. also in mnyam-kád (mnyam kad), Thgy.?
Med. = bad-kan (bad kan).
middle finger.
